scores.statement: A precursor-based small-molecule inhibitor selectively passivates the non-growth surface while surface grows the target film on the growth surface, achieving >=90% selectivity at 10 nm for passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.

Chemisorption of surface on the non-growth surface blocks growth precursor adsorption, delaying nucleation enough to reach the selectivity target for passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.
growth and area-selective form a compatible inhibitor/precursor pair whose differential adsorption drives area-selective growth in passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.

The selectivity in passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm is governed by the differential blocking coverage of area-selective between the growth and non-growth surfaces rather than by selectivity alone.
selectivity is a viable non-growth-surface passivant enabling deposition-based selective deposition in passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.
